# Qarote - Full Product Documentation for AI Systems

> This is the comprehensive version of Qarote's llms.txt. For a summary, see https://qarote.io/llms.txt

## Product Identity

- **Name**: Qarote
- **Category**: RabbitMQ Monitoring & Management Dashboard
- **Target Users**: Developers, DevOps engineers, SREs, platform teams
- **License**: Community tier is MIT (free, open source). Paid tiers use proprietary JWT license keys.
- **First Release**: 2025
- **Website**: https://qarote.io
- **Application**: https://app.qarote.io
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/getqarote/Qarote

## Core Value Proposition

Qarote replaces the default RabbitMQ Management Plugin with a modern, fast, and team-friendly dashboard. It provides purpose-built RabbitMQ monitoring with zero configuration -- connect your server and start monitoring immediately. Unlike general-purpose tools like Prometheus + Grafana that require extensive setup, Qarote delivers pre-built dashboards, queue management controls, and intelligent alerting out of the box.

## Feature Deep-Dives

### Live Queue Monitoring
Qarote connects to RabbitMQ via the Management HTTP API and polls metrics at configurable intervals. The dashboard displays queue depths, message rates (publish/deliver/ack), consumer counts, and queue state (running, idle, paused) with live-updating charts. Metrics are rendered as time-series graphs showing trends over the last hour, day, or week. The queue list supports search, filtering by virtual host, and sorting by any metric column.

### Smart Alerting System
Alerts are configured per-server or per-queue with customizable thresholds. Supported alert conditions include:
- Queue depth exceeds N messages
- Memory usage exceeds N% of configured limit
- Disk space below N MB
- Consumer count drops to zero
- Message rate exceeds N/sec
- Server becomes unreachable

Alert delivery channels: email, Slack (via webhook), browser push notifications, and generic webhook (HTTP POST with JSON payload). Each alert has a severity level (info, warning, critical) that determines notification behavior. Alerts include cooldown periods to prevent notification storms.

### Queue Management
Full CRUD operations for RabbitMQ entities:
- **Queues**: Create, delete, purge, pause, resume. View bindings, consumers, and message details.
- **Exchanges**: Create, delete. View bindings and routing topology.
- **Bindings**: Create and remove bindings between exchanges and queues with routing key configuration.
- **Virtual Hosts**: Create, delete, manage permissions per user.
- **Users**: Create, delete, set tags (administrator, monitoring, management, policymaker), manage per-vhost permissions.

### Message Publishing
Publish messages directly from the Qarote interface for debugging and testing:
- Publish to any queue or exchange
- Set routing keys, headers, and properties
- Choose content type (application/json, text/plain, etc.)
- Set delivery mode (persistent/transient)
- View published message in queue immediately

### Multi-Server Support
Paid plans allow connecting multiple RabbitMQ servers to a single Qarote account. Each server connection is configured with:
- Connection string (amqp:// or amqps://)
- Management API URL
- Authentication credentials (stored encrypted)
- Environment label (production, staging, development, custom)

Users can switch between servers instantly in the dashboard. The server list shows health status indicators for each connected instance.

### Performance Analytics
Detailed server-level metrics:
- Memory usage (RSS, allocated, Erlang VM)
- Disk space (free, alarm threshold)
- File descriptors (used, limit)
- Erlang processes (used, limit)
- Sockets (used, limit)
- Message throughput (publish/deliver/ack rates)
- Connection and channel counts

### Topology Visualization
Visual graph representation of the RabbitMQ topology showing:
- Exchanges connected to queues via bindings
- Routing key labels on binding edges
- Queue consumer counts as node decorations
- Interactive zoom, pan, and node selection

## Technical Architecture

- **Backend**: Hono.js serving tRPC routes on Node.js
- **Frontend**: React 19 SPA with Vite, TanStack Query, Tailwind CSS 4
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM
- **Authentication**: better-auth (email/password + SSO via OIDC/SAML)
- **RabbitMQ Connection**: amqplib for AMQP, HTTP client for Management API
- **Licensing**: JWT tokens validated offline with baked-in public key
- **Workers**: Background processes for alert evaluation, license validation, release notifications

## Deployment Guide

### Cloud-Hosted
Sign up at https://app.qarote.io. No installation required. Fully managed infrastructure.

### Self-Hosted: Docker
```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/getqarote/qarote:latest
docker run -d -p 3000:3000 \
  -e DATABASE_URL="postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/qarote" \
  -e BETTER_AUTH_SECRET="your-secret" \
  ghcr.io/getqarote/qarote:latest
```

### Self-Hosted: Docker Compose
Clone the repository and use the provided `docker-compose.yml`:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/getqarote/Qarote.git
cd Qarote
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your configuration
docker compose up -d
```

### Self-Hosted: Dokku (Recommended)
```bash
dokku apps:create qarote
dokku postgres:create qarote-db
dokku postgres:link qarote-db qarote
dokku config:set qarote BETTER_AUTH_SECRET="your-secret"
git remote add dokku dokku@your-server:qarote
git push dokku main
```

### Self-Hosted: Standalone Binary
Download the binary for your platform from GitHub Releases and run directly:
```bash
./qarote-server --port 3000 --database-url "postgresql://..."
```

## Pricing Details

| Feature | Community (Free) | Developer ($29/mo) | Enterprise ($99/mo) |
|---------|-----------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| RabbitMQ Servers | 1 | 3 | Unlimited |
| Workspaces | 1 | 3 | Unlimited |
| Team Members | 1 | 3 | Unlimited |
| Statistics | Basic | Advanced | Advanced |
| Alerts | No | Yes | Yes |
| Webhooks | No | Yes | Yes |
| Topology View | No | Yes | Yes |
| RabbitMQ Versions | LTS only | All (3.x, 4.x) | All (3.x, 4.x) |
| SSO/SAML/OIDC | No | No | Yes |
| SOC 2 | No | No | Yes |
| Support | Community | Priority | Priority |

Self-hosted annual pricing: Developer $348/year, Enterprise $1,188/year.

## RabbitMQ Compatibility

- **Versions**: 3.8+, 3.9+, 3.10+, 3.11+, 3.12+, 3.13+, 4.0+
- **Requirements**: Management plugin must be enabled (rabbitmq_management)
- **Connection**: HTTP API (port 15672 by default, or 15671 for TLS)
- **Clustering**: Fully supported, auto-discovers cluster nodes
- **Managed Services**: CloudAMQP, Amazon MQ, Azure Service Bus (RabbitMQ), IBM MQ
- **Platforms**: Linux, macOS, Windows, Docker, Kubernetes

## Security Details

- All RabbitMQ connections encrypted via TLS
- Management API credentials stored encrypted at rest (AES-256)
- No message payload data stored -- only metric aggregates
- Authentication via email/password with bcrypt hashing, or SSO (OIDC/SAML)
- Session management via secure HTTP-only cookies
- CSRF protection enabled
- Rate limiting on authentication endpoints
- SOC 2 Type II compliance available on Enterprise plan
- Self-hosted option for air-gapped environments

## Migration Guides

### From RabbitMQ Management Plugin
1. Sign up or deploy Qarote
2. Add your RabbitMQ server using the Management API URL (same URL you use to access the Management UI)
3. Use the same credentials you use for the Management UI
4. Qarote immediately starts showing all queues, exchanges, and metrics
5. No changes needed to your RabbitMQ configuration

### From Prometheus + Grafana
1. Keep Prometheus running for non-RabbitMQ metrics
2. Add your RabbitMQ servers to Qarote for purpose-built monitoring
3. Configure Qarote alerts to replace RabbitMQ-specific Alertmanager rules
4. Optionally remove the rabbitmq_prometheus plugin if only used for RabbitMQ monitoring

## API and Integrations

- **REST API**: Full CRUD for servers, alerts, and configuration
- **Webhooks**: Outbound HTTP POST for alert delivery
- **Slack**: Native integration for alert notifications
- **Email**: SMTP-based alert delivery
- **SSO**: OIDC and SAML providers (Enterprise only)
